item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That implication that the Foundation tested this on a pregnant woman just to see what would happen.

item.title: Re: Staff Post -- Open
item.content:encoded: Thanks for catching that.

item.title: Re: Staff Post -- Open
item.content:encoded: *temporal

item.title: Staff Post -- Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at anytime. ~ Conwell

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show whatever
- Hide whatever

I'm very curious as to how the permanently changed individuals will react if they were to be anomalously transformed back into their age. And so, I feel that the Foundation would also be interested in how these individuals would react if transformed into another age. So, I would recommend adding the following at the end of the article.

Crosstesting between several different temporal based anomalies is currently pending the approval of the Site-24 director. These include, but are not limited to, SCP-559, SCP-983, and SCP-1080.

Additionally, I would recommend including a change from this:

The packaging reads "Dr. Wondertainment's Insta-teen Tablets™".

To the following:

The packaging reads "Dr. Wondertainment's Insta-teen Tablets™".

If you have a crosslink suggestion, please let me know!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ^This

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very good. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What caught my interest, and why you get an "omigawd" upvote is something a little more subtle.

These changes are irreversible once the subject reverts.

So, if you OD on these little wonders, you are an 18-19 year old forever?

Wow.

Would that be only mental/emotional or would all the bodily changes persist as well? As in, not only the acne, but the general physical appearance and physiology, too?

There's a Tale in that, for sure. Would love to read it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought the bit about the pregnant woman was pure fridge brilliance. It didn't hint me until later: The Foundation had to go and find a pregnant Class D female to test that out on just to find out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Colourful foetuses are my favourite centre of horrouor

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I knew that Corbette wrote this when I read Foetus. Another good one man, good job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How this process occurs is not understood, however the change is extensive; fat is moved to the lips and buttocks, bones become less brittle, muscles become more toned, and hair is regrown.

And the unintended implications of this is why I upvoted.
Hell, even with normal weight men or women, their fat moving to lips and buttocks … never mind overweight or obese.
The results of taking one of these must be grotesque.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think this really seems like something that Dr. Wondertainment would do, but that wasn't really enough to keep me from upvoting. (It might have if I was able to explain my position a little better, but I can't ^^; sorry.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked the article but felt it just could be developed further. It doesn't have that hook, and while the image of a teenager bursting from another teenager gives some of that hook, it's largely left underdeveloped. Neutral Vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked the ending, wondered if the presentation could be more interesting. The range of effects could be expanded on, a bit. Neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Willy Wonka -did- have age-changing candies! They showed up as an extensive subplot in Charlie and the Great Glass Elevator. They were used on his grandparents and one of his grandmother snuck extras and embryoed herself…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I imagine they immediately go to the overdose effects.

"No apparent effect upon teenagers Individuals between the ages of 14 and 20 cease to mentally or physiologically progress. In effect, they remain teenagers forever, with attendant social maturity."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not mentioning the effects on teenagers is a definite oversight. Also, why 14 as a cutoff? Shouldn't it be 12 or younger?

item.title: Re: Not bad...
item.content:encoded: DID YOU FINISH THE PAPERWORK STEVE?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Only the ending saved it from a downvote.

item.title: Re: Not bad...
item.content:encoded: Steve works for the CIA?

item.title: Re: Not bad...
item.content:encoded:
That's not a thing that exists. Do you mean the Factory?

No man THE COMPANY

Edit: Steve works there

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This idea(pills that age up or age down the person that takes them) is a common one in children's fantasy and an object that is very suitable as something from Dr. Wondertainment. There is also some very potent Fridge Horror that can be brought to the fore by including something like this:

SCP-1642 was brought to the Foundations attention after an incident in suburban █████████,█████████ where 3 unidentified individuals, 2 males (teenage) and 1 female (teenage, deceased), were found in a backyard treehouse by Mr. ██████████. One of the unidentified males later reverted and was identified as ██████████ ██████████(age 11), Mr ██████████ son. The deceased female was genetically identified as █████ █████████(age 9), a child resident of the neighborhood that went missing around the time of the incident. The 2 male reverted to a foetus and was determined by genetic testing to be the child of ██████████ ██████████ and █████ █████████.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Funny, it doesn't seem tacked-on to me. It makes logical sense from the effects given.

item.title: Re: Not bad...
item.content:encoded:
This might work better from The Company, at least in my opinion.

You mean The Factory? If so, I agree.

Personally I feel "enhanced" has too positive a connotation. I think "uncontrollable" is acceptable, but I'm not the author here, so whatevs. I was thinking that anorexia being a common side effect was part of the anomaly, but it did feel a bit of a ham-handed nod towards archetypal teen issues.

In my opinion, the length is fine. We need more short, simple SCPs that aren't trying to add on as much as possible as they can to their concepts.

item.title: Re: Not bad...
item.content:encoded:
The Company

That's not a thing that exists. Do you mean the Factory?

"uncontrollable" (that's not a clinical term, anyway)

"Uncontrollable" is a clinical term for describing things that cannot be controlled.

anorexia nervosa really isn't that common

In real life. It might be more common as a symptom of these sweets than naturally occuring in adolecents.

item.title: Not bad...
item.content:encoded:
It's… interesting, but…

1. I'm not sure this fits Wondertainment. Wondertainment is definitely (and defiantly) child-oriented, as with the amusement park, Robo-Dude, etc.; Wondertainment products aren't usually for adults. This might work better from The Company, at least in my opinion.

2. The overdose effects need a bit of work. The libido shouldn't be "uncontrollable" (that's not a clinical term, anyway) but "enhanced". Likewise, anorexia nervosa really isn't that common; the highest known rate of incidence is 1% in women, 0.1% in men.

3. The article feels a bit short, to be honest. It could use a recovery report, at least; as written, testing logs wouldn't provide much of interest, and a suitable picture might be hard to come by.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're still using "embryo" where it should be "foetus" according to the previous sentence. +1 for concept but please fix that, other people might downvote for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree. Needs something…logs or interviews, perhaps. It still has a sandboxy feel but it could be really good. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad. The squick at the end was enough to earn my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm hesitant to ask – what happens if a teenager takes one of these tablets? [DATA EXPUNGED]?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really don't know what to make of this, but i'm upvoting because I like the concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A bit extreme, but definitely not bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What everyone else said. I like the idea enough that I won't downvote, but I can't say that I like the writing and effect enough to board the upboat. Also, the bit about pregnant women seems tacked-on for extra squick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty much everything in the SCP follows from the name of the product — nothing felt significantly unexpected. I generally like to be surprised at least a little by an SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not bad, but nothing unexpected. No vote.

Edit: Nahwhat? I think I like this as it is. Short, simple squick that doesn't have to try to be anything more. Upvoted.

Edit: Actually, this feels more like a Factory Item. Still upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This feels like a generic "easy-to-misuse" Wondertainment item. I like the idea of a mewling, toddling teenager, but I can't vote on this right now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The SCP doesn't expand significantly on this line

What do you mean by this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Has potential. Needs work. No vote yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The packaging reads "Dr. Wondertaniment®'s Insta-teen Tablets™".

The SCP doesn't expand significantly on this line, and the squick doesn't carry it. Not by a long shot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm, you have a point. Perhaps I should change it over to Prometheus labs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The squick is good, but I couldn't help but think, "Willy Wonka, is that you?"

I mean…Dr. Wondertainment's always had that sort of mischievous wonka-esque atmosphere, but…this takes it in the wrong-ish direction for me. I dunno. Maybe it's because I've seen the age-related-pill somewhere before, but I'm just not sure exactly where…

Anyways, neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nothing like some good ol' squick to keep you awake for the night. This one was written in about 10 minutes without consulting anybody else about the idea or anything, run through the chat, then posted. A good example of some classic SCP processing.
